revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Anesaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Anesaki-jinja, haiden.JPG | location = {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan | ISO = JP-12 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 24.32 | longitude_deg = 140 | longitude_min = 2 | longitude_sec = 53.38 | deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (minor), Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | established = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} | festivals = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|qid=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}} | map = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Ichihara|qid=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.473428|N|140.048161|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Anesaki Shrine''' ({{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|姉埼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}}, Anesaki-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. While the local area name is written as "姉崎" (Anesaki), the shrine’s name is written as "姉埼" (Anesaki). == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following five deities:Shrine historical pamphlet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|en|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (Shinatobe-no-Mikoto) - A god of wind. The shrine’s tradition identifies this deity as either the wife or elder sister of the deity enshrined at {{ill|Shimana Shrine|ja|島穴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimana Shrine|1=Shimaana Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11477020}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} (Yamato Takeru no Mikoto) :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto) :* {{ill|Kunado-no-Kami|en|Kunado-no-Kami|qid=Q2963653|lt=Kuni-no-Sakimatsuri|1=Kunado-no-Kami}} (Saenomihashira no Kami) :* Ōsasaki no Mikoto (大雀命) - Identified as Emperor {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. == History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) dispatched {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} to suppress revolts in the eastern provinces. The texts describe a storm that endangered Yamato Takeru’s crossing at {{ill|Uraga Channel|en|Uraga Channel|qid=Q723431|lt=Uraga Channel|1=Uraga Channel}} until his wife, Princess {{ill|Ototachibana|ja|弟橘姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ototachibana|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487396}}, sacrificed herself to calm the seas. According to shrine lore, Yamato Takeru subsequently enshrined Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, a wind deity, at the present shrine location in memory of his wife. Later legends recount Emperor Keikō’s visit to the shrine to honor his son Yamato Takeru. Further additions to the shrine’s pantheon are attributed to successive generations of the {{ill|Shanghai-kamiko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanghai-kamiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan, with key events recorded during the reigns of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} (13th emperor) and Emperor {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|qid=Q329704|lt=Ritchū|1=Emperor Richū}} (17th emperor). Although these traditions remain unverifiable, the area around the shrine, known for the {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}}, suggests the region was a power center for the Shanghai-kamiko clan.{{harvid|Anesaki Shrine・Shimana Shrine (Kami)|1984|qq=}} === Historical development === The shrine is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as receiving divine Rankings in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}: * In {{ill|877|en|877|qid=Q23905|lt=877|1=877}}, it was elevated to "Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade." * In {{ill|884|en|884|qid=Q23895|lt=884|1=884}}, it was elevated to "Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade." The shrine is also listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} of {{ill|927|en|927|qid=Q30470|lt=927|1=927}}.{{harvid|Anesaki Shrine・Shimana Shrine (Kami)|1984|qq=}} In modern times, the shrine achieved {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} status during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. However, a fire in {{ill|1986|en|1986|qid=Q2430|lt=Showa 61|1=1986}} destroyed its main building and sacred tree. Both have since been restored. === Divine Rankings === * {{ill|877|en|877|qid=Q23905|lt=877|1=877}}: "Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade" (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku). * {{ill|884|en|884|qid=Q23895|lt=884|1=884}}: Promoted to "Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade." == Precincts == File:Anesaki-jinja, shaden.JPG|Shrine building
{{small|The connected structure includes the haiden and honden.|qq=}} File:Anesaki-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main shrine building) File:姉埼神社 正面鳥居.JPG|Main torii gate == Subsidiary Shrines == Several smaller shrines, including an Asama Shrine on a {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|qid=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka|1=Fujizuka}}, are located within the precincts. File:姉埼神社 富士塚.JPG|Asama Shrine (on Fujizuka) File:姉埼神社 摂末社.JPG|Subsidiary shrines == Legends == The shrine precincts notably lack pine trees, and this absence is linked to local legends explaining the shrine’s name and customs: * Shinatobe-no-Mikoto, mourning the prolonged absence of Shinatsuhiko, lamented, "Waiting is hard," leading to the taboo against "matsu" (pine, but also "to wait"). * The place name "Anesaki" is said to originate from the elder sister (ane) awaiting her younger brother (saki) in this location. In the shrine's surrounding area, New Year’s decorations traditionally avoid using pine. Instead, branches of {{ill|sakaki|ja|榊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sakaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サカキ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サカキ|1=Cleyera japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q775470}} are used, referred to as "kado-sakaki." == Access == '''Address:''' * Anesaki 2270, Ichihara, Chiba Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation:''' * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Anegasaki Station|en|Anegasaki Station|qid=Q573432|lt=Anegasaki Station|1=Anegasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Uchibō Line|en|Uchibō Line|qid=Q1142964|lt=Uchibō Line|1=Uchibō Line}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q499071}}. '''Nearby landmarks:''' * {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}} - Includes {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|姉崎天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Footnotes|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == * Shrine historical pamphlet, ''Anesaki Shrine Visitor’s Guide''. * {{Cite book|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|title={{ill|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|ja|府県郷社明治神社誌料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486103}}|year=1912|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=[[Ken'ichi Tanigawa]]|author=Suzuki Nakaaki|title=Japan's Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites|year=1984|publisher=Hakusuisha|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine・Shimana Shrine (Kami)|1984|qq=}}|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimana Shrine|ja|島穴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimana Shrine|1=Shimaana Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11477020}} * {{ill|Shanghai-kamiko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanghai-kamiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Anesaki-jinja|qq=}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2030 Anesaki Shrine] - Chiba Shrine Bureau official website {{Shinto Horizontal|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|姉埼神社|version=101197973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Anesaki-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Anesaki Shrine (Q11447235)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kazusa Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Unakami district|ja|海上郡 (上総国)|qid=Q11558726|1=Unakami district}} || {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|lt=Anesaki Shrine|ja|姉埼神社|commons|Category:Anesaki-jinja|sv|Anesaki-jinja|qid=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.473409|140.048357|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181741 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11447235}} [[da:d:Q11447235]] [[ja:姉埼神社]] [[sv:Anesaki-jinja]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]]==Notes== [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Fujizuka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ichihara, Chiba]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]]
